summary: Platform Application Capabilities Kit (PACK) is a TypeScript monorepo providing an ALPHA-state framework for building collaborative, real-time document-based applications on Palantir Foundry. It provides a complete application SDK with document schemas (Zod-validated models), real-time state synchronization via Yjs CRDTs, presence/activity event broadcasting, OAuth authentication against Foundry, and React hooks for UI integration. The framework centers on a 'Document' abstraction rather than direct Ontology manipulation, but bridges to Foundry's Ontology via OSDK client integration, ontologyRid configuration, and ObjectRef types for referencing Ontology objects within documents.

ontologyConcepts.description: AppConfig stores ontologyRid as Promise<string>; every PACK app is scoped to a Foundry Ontology via its RID. The getOntologyRid utility resolves it from the app instance. Format: ri.ontology.main.ontology.<uuid>.

PACK apps are initialized with an OSDK Client instance (from @osdk/client). The osdkClient is stored on AppConfig and used for Foundry API communication. OSDK is Palantir's Ontology SDK for TypeScript.

ObjectRef is a branded reference type pointing to an Ontology object (ObjectId). Marked @experimental with TODO for implementation. Allows document schemas to reference Ontology objects as field values within document records.

ontologyConcepts.concept: DocumentRef / Document as collaborative state container
ontologyConcepts.description: DocumentRef is the central abstraction: a reference to a collaborative, real-time document. While not an Ontology object itself, documents exist within an Ontology-scoped context and can contain references to Ontology objects (ObjectRef), users (UserRef), media (MediaRef), and other documents (DocRef).

ontologyConcepts.concept: External reference types (DocRef, MediaRef, ObjectRef, UserRef)
ontologyConcepts.description: The Model metadata tracks which fields are external references via externalRefFieldTypes. ExternalRefType enum: DOC_REF, MEDIA_REF, OBJECT_REF, USER_REF. ObjectRef specifically bridges to Ontology objects.
The state service connects to Foundry via WebSocket at /api/v2/packSubscriptions for real-time document sync. This is a Foundry platform API for the Pack system.
The Foundry state implementations depend on @osdk/foundry.pack (v2.47.0), a generated OSDK package providing typed access to Foundry's Pack service APIs.

packages.name: @palantir/pack.core
packages.description: Core types and utilities: PackApp, AppConfig, ModuleKey, TokenProvider, branded types (Flavored), getOntologyRid, generateId, parseJwtPayload

packages.name: @palantir/pack.app
packages.description: Main application package: initPackApp, page environment detection, demo OAuth client

packages.name: @palantir/pack.auth
packages.description: Authentication core: AuthModule, AuthState, UserRef, token management

packages.name: @palantir/pack.auth.foundry
packages.description: Foundry OAuth implementations: public, confidential, and static token auth services

packages.name: @palantir/pack.schema
packages.description: Schema definition DSL: defineRecord, defineUnion, defineMigration, primitive types (String, Double, Boolean, Array, Optional, DocRef, MediaRef, ObjectRef, UserRef, Unknown)

packages.name: @palantir/pack.document-schema.model-types
packages.description: Runtime model types supporting generated schemas: DocumentSchema, DocumentRef, Model, RecordRef, RecordCollectionRef, branded ref types, activity/presence events

packages.name: @palantir/pack.document-schema.type-gen
packages.description: CLI code generator: converts YAML schema definitions to TypeScript types and Zod validation schemas via an intermediate representation (IR)

packages.name: @palantir/pack.state.core
packages.description: State management core: DocumentService interface, StateModule, Yjs-based base service, document lifecycle (create, search, load, subscribe)

packages.name: @palantir/pack.state.foundry
packages.description: Foundry-backed DocumentService implementation using Foundry Pack APIs and conjure-client

packages.name: @palantir/pack.state.foundry-event
packages.description: Real-time event service for Foundry document sync via CometD (Bayeux protocol) and WebSocket

packages.name: @palantir/pack.state.demo
packages.description: Demo/test implementation of DocumentService using in-browser BroadcastChannel for local-only collaborative editing

packages.name: @palantir/pack.state.react
packages.description: React hooks and providers for PACK state: document references, records, search, presence, activity, auth

packages.name: @palantir/pack.sdkgen
packages.description: Generic TypeScript SDK generator CLI: creates project scaffolding from templates using EJS, commander, and inquirer

architecturePatterns.description: PACK introduces a 'Document' abstraction as its primary state container, distinct from Ontology objects. Documents have typed schemas (via Zod), contain Records (key-value entries within named collections), and support real-time collaborative editing. This is a higher-level construct built atop Foundry's storage layer.

State synchronization uses Yjs (CRDT library) as the conflict-resolution layer. BaseYjsDocumentService provides the base implementation, with Foundry and demo backends plugging in different transport layers. This enables Google-Docs-style collaborative editing.

PackApp uses a ModuleKey-based module system. Modules (auth, state) register via ModuleConfig tuples and are accessed via module accessors added dynamically during initialization. Declaration merging extends the PackApp interface.

A multi-stage code generation pipeline: YAML schema definitions -> steps -> intermediate representation (IR) -> TypeScript types + Zod validation schemas. The type-gen CLI processes schemas through this pipeline. Separate sdkgen CLI generates full project scaffolding from templates.

Uses branded types (Flavored<T>) for type-safe IDs: DocumentId, RecordId, ObjectId, MediaId, UserId. Symbol-based branding (DocumentRefBrand, ObjectRefBrand, etc.) prevents accidental reference type confusion.

Core interfaces (DocumentService, AuthService, EventService) are backend-agnostic. Two implementations exist: Foundry (production, uses real APIs) and Demo (local, uses BroadcastChannel/IndexedDB). Auto-detection via isDemoEnv/demoMode config.
The foundry-event package uses CometD (Bayeux protocol) over WebSocket at /api/v2/packSubscriptions for real-time presence and activity event streaming from Foundry.

architecturePatterns.pattern: React hooks layer for UI integration
architecturePatterns.description: pack.state.react provides idiomatic React hooks (useDocRef, useRecord, useRecords, useSearchDocuments, etc.) wrapping the imperative DocumentService API, with a PackAppProvider context.

typeDefinitions.name: PackApp
typeDefinitions.description: Top-level application instance providing access to config and dynamically-added modules (auth, state). Created via initPackApp().

typeDefinitions.name: AppConfig
typeDefinitions.description: Application configuration: app identity (appId, appVersion), OSDK client, ontologyRid (Promise<string>), remote endpoints (baseUrl, packWsPath), logger, demoMode flag.

typeDefinitions.name: DocumentSchema
typeDefinitions.description: Interface for generated document schemas. Maps model names to Model instances. Includes version metadata. DocumentState<S> derives the plain object representation.

typeDefinitions.name: Model
typeDefinitions.description: Defines the structure of a document record or union. Contains Zod schema for validation, metadata (name, externalRefFieldTypes for records, discriminant for unions).

typeDefinitions.name: DocumentRef
typeDefinitions.description: Branded reference to a collaborative document. Provides methods: getDocSnapshot, getRecords, onActivity, onMetadataChange, onPresence, onStateChange, updateCustomPresence, withTransaction.

typeDefinitions.name: DocumentService
typeDefinitions.description: Core service interface for document operations: createDocument, searchDocuments, createDocRef, record CRUD, collection operations, subscriptions for activity/presence/status/metadata changes.

typeDefinitions.name: RecordDef / UnionDef
typeDefinitions.description: Schema DSL definition types. RecordDef has named typed fields, UnionDef has named variants with a discriminant field. Used as input to the code generation pipeline.

typeDefinitions.name: ObjectRef
typeDefinitions.description: Branded reference to an Ontology object (ObjectId). Marked @experimental. Bridge type enabling documents to reference Ontology objects.

typeDefinitions.name: ExternalRefType
typeDefinitions.description: Enum of external reference field types: DOC_REF, MEDIA_REF, OBJECT_REF, USER_REF. Used in RecordModelMetadata to annotate which fields hold cross-entity references.
